en: And we've been talking about when will 50% of engineers in the world be writing 100% AI code?
zh: 我们一直在谈论，全球 50% 的工程师什么时候会编写 100% 的 AI 代码？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's probably in a year, which is insane how much that job has changed.
zh: 可能是一年内，那份工作改变了多少真是太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Which role do you think AI transforms next?
zh: 您认为 AI 下一个会转变哪个角色？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it marketing?
zh: 是市场营销吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it growth?
zh: 是增长吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it sales?
zh: 是销售吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it design?
zh: 是设计吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a sense of where things are starting to really change other than engineering?
zh: 您对哪些领域除了工程之外开始真正发生变化有感觉吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is maybe a hot take and I actually don't have enough ...
zh: 这可能是一个激进的观点，实际上我也没有足够的……

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very likely that the labs are like, "Ha ha, look at this guy."
zh: 实验室很可能是像，“哈哈，看看这家伙。”

speaker: Unknown
en: My take, it's very clear at least empirically that models are getting better at coding at some exponential rate.
zh: 我的看法，至少在经验上很清楚，模型正在以某种指数级的速度变得更好地编写代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't think that's changing.
zh: 我认为这不会改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, I'm not that impressed with the progress in any other domain.
zh: 现在，我对任何其他领域的进步并没有那么印象深刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: It tends to be ...
zh: 倾向于……

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think they've gotten significantly better at writing.
zh: 我认为它们在写作方面并没有显著变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I still very much hate reading AI slop writing.
zh: 我仍然非常讨厌阅读 AI 垃圾写作。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the thing is software, Andreessen, software is eating the world.
zh: 但事情是软件，Andreessen，软件正在吞噬世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, if the cost of software and creating software and encoding business practices in code, and I just literally mean the old software 1.0 kind of code, then if that cost is very much going to zero, we will just have a lot more of it.
zh: 好吧，如果软件的成本，创建软件的成本，以及将商业实践编码到代码中的成本，我只是字面上指旧的软件 1.0 类型的代码，那么如果那个成本非常趋近于零，我们只会拥有更多它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think then in that case, it's more that software engineering will go into all the other domains, not necessarily that there is some sort of ... Yeah, I don't know.
zh: 所以我认为那样的话，更多的是软件工程将进入所有其他领域，不一定有某种……是的，我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our folks in HR are automating a lot of things because now, they don't have to bug an engineering team to write that code.
zh: 我们 HR 的人正在自动化很多事情，因为现在，他们不需要去烦工程团队来编写那个代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that's how it's going.
zh: 所以我认为事情就是这样发展的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you look at when the model companies say, "Oh, we've made great progress in this other non-coding domain," I was like, "You just applied coding principles to this domain, which is wonderful, but that's what it's getting better at."
zh: 如果你看当模型公司说，“哦，我们在这个其他非编码领域取得了巨大进步，”我会说，“您只是将编码原则应用到了这个领域，这很棒，但这正是它变得更好的地方。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I just think software is eating the world is going to accelerate.
zh: 所以我只是认为软件正在吞噬世界将会加速。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is a really interesting take.
zh: 这是一个非常有趣的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's basically software, just the acceleration of software eating the world versus it's like, it's going to now do a different kind of job.
zh: 所以基本上是软件，只是软件吞噬世界的加速，而不是像，它现在要做一种不同类型的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: This makes me think about the head of product for Codex said the same thing that every agent, there's all these different kinds of agents and his take is every agent that will win is going to be a coding agent that builds the thing it needs versus it's come, it has certain number of capabilities.
zh: 这让我想到 Codex 的产品负责人说同样的话，每个智能体，有所有这些不同种类的智能体，他的观点是每个会赢的智能体都将是一个构建它所需要东西的编码智能体，而不是它来，它有一定数量的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: OpenCloud's such a good example.
zh: OpenCloud 就是一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just like, I will build a skill for myself and now I know how to do this thing.
zh: 就像，我将为自己构建一个技能，现在我知道如何做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: All agents are also, if you look at all the harnesses, whether it's the open source ones or the ones from the model companies, ours as well, they all resemble a coding agent now.
zh: 所有的智能体也是，如果你看所有的套具，无论是开源的还是来自模型公司的，我们的也是，它们现在都 resemble 一个编码智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to come back to the ROI piece.
zh: 我要回到 ROI 这一块。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think this is really interesting.
zh: 我认为这真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, there's just like, okay, we're going to spend, spend, spend, just see what happens, learn, accelerate, lean into all this stuff.
zh: 正如您所说，就像，好吧，我们要花、花、花，看看会发生什么，学习、加速、投入所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're saying that in maybe six months, something like that, you think a lot of companies are going to start really looking at the cost here.
zh: 您是说在大概六个月左右，类似那样，您认为很多公司将开始真正审视这里的成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know you said you don't like to predict things, but what do you predict is going to start happening?
zh: 我知道您说过您不喜欢预测事情，但您预测将会开始发生什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I probably spend too much time than I should because I have literally zero impact on any of this as how it plays out, but you can imagine a world where the labs, the delta between the labs and open weight models and so on widens.
zh: 我可能花费了太多的时间，因为我对这一切如何发展实际上零影响，但你可以想象一个实验室，实验室和开源权重模型之间的差距等等扩大的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is a world that I very much don't like because I hate centralization of power.
zh: 那是一个非常我不喜欢的世界，因为我讨厌权力的中心化。

speaker: Unknown
en: But in that world, I think the labs just get to decide what the world looks like.
zh: 但在那个世界里，我认为实验室可以决定世界的样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think if that gap doesn't widen, then you will just see a diffusion and people will get very comfortable running their own models, RLing their own models.
zh: 我认为如果那个差距不拉大，那么你只会看到一种扩散，人们会非常从容地运行自己的模型，对自己的模型进行 RL。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like you see this with Cursor, you see this with Intercom, Notion is dabbling in it as well.
zh: 就像你在 Cursor 上看到的，你在 Intercom 上看到的，Notion 也在涉足其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Use dabbling right now, but obviously at some point we might become more serious about it.
zh: 现在用涉足这个词，但显然在某个时刻我们可能会更认真地对待它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you have, it may not be the frontier, but for a lot of tasks, it'll be good enough.
zh: 然后你会得到，它可能不是最前沿的，但对于很多任务来说，它会足够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think in that case, that is just an ROI calculation.
zh: 所以我认为在这种情况下，这只是一个 ROI 计算。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the, is it cheaper for me to send this task to a smaller model that is cheaper to run where remove the lab profit margin kind of thing?
zh: 那就是，对我来说，把这个任务发送给一个运行成本更小、更便宜的小型模型是不是更便宜，这就像去掉了实验室利润 margins 之类的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that may happen, but it only happens if there isn't a fast sort of like, oh yeah, the gap is now so big.
zh: 我认为这可能会发生，但这只有在没有一种快速的情况时才会发生，比如，噢耶，差距现在太大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other one that's interesting is right now, I think we're actually in one of the luckiest possible timelines, which is we have, at least in the US, three competent labs that are all sort of duking it out at the ... and like who knows, maybe Meta now, so four.
zh: 另一个有趣的是，现在，我认为我们实际上处于最幸运的时间线之一，也就是我们至少在美国有三家有能力 的实验室都在……谁知道呢，也许 Meta 现在也算一家，所以是四家。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe we can make it six at some point.
zh: 也许我们在某个时候能让它变成六家。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think I would love a world where we have a dozen frontier models in the US versus having to always rely on other places in the world to do this, but that's pretty good.
zh: 我希望在美国有十几个前沿模型的世界，而不是总是不得不依赖世界其他地方来做这件事，但这已经相当不错了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If that stopped, I would be somewhat worried.
zh: 如果这停止了，我会有些担心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it's hard to predict what would happen.
zh: 然后很难预测会发生什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if that doesn't, then I think it's going to look similar to the cloud wars, which is at some point layers commoditize, businesses are not going to want to lock in into one single provider.
zh: 但如果没有停止，那么我认为它看起来会类似于云战争，也就是在某些时候层 商品化了，企业不会想锁定到单一提供商。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the past life, I worked at Heroku and Kubernetes was much more successful than Heroku, even though I think from a user experience perspective, it was much worse.
zh: 在以前的生活中，我在 Heroku 工作，而 Kubernetes 比 Heroku 成功得多，尽管我认为从用户体验的角度来看，它要差得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the delta was Heroku was saying, "Hey, we're going to replace your ops team."
zh: 但差异在于 Heroku 说，“嘿，我们要取代你的运维 团队。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And Kubernetes was, "We're going to make your ops team superheroes.
zh: 而 Kubernetes 是，“我们要让你的运维 团队成为超级英雄。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also, we're not going to lock you into a cloud, you can choose."
zh: 而且，我们不会把你锁定在一个云里，你可以选择。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And obviously, that's what businesses want.
zh: 显然，这就是企业想要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Businesses want choice.
zh: 企业想要选择。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I don't know.
zh: 所以我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really hard to predict because it depends.
zh: 真的很难预测，因为它取决于具体情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's so asymmetric in terms of model progress.
zh: 所以在模型进展方面它是如此不对称。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you say the products that win often are the ones that make you feel like superheroes, I always think about Kathy Sierra.
zh: 当你说获胜的产品往往是那些让你感觉像超级英雄的产品时，我总是会想到 Kathy Sierra。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you remember that at all as a thing?
zh: 你还记得这回事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: It rings a bell.
zh: 听起来很耳熟。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like from the open days at this point shows how old I am.
zh: 这就像是从开源 的日子开始的，这表明我有多老了。

speaker: Unknown
en: She was just something that really stuck with me and I think it's informed a lot of how people think about product, at least in the past is just her whole pitch was instead of talking about your product and how amazing it is, it's about we'll make you a superhero.
zh: 她只是真的让我印象深刻的一件事，我认为这影响了很多人们对产品的思考，至少在过去是，她的整个推介不是谈论你的产品以及它有多棒，而是我们要让你成为超级英雄。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like Mario getting the little flower and having superpowers now versus look at her incredible product.
zh: 这就像马里奥得到了那个小花并拥有了超能力，而不是看她那令人难以置信的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's actually a thing that the coding companies had to learn when they tried to move to like, why do code review tools, automatic code review tools not work that well?
zh: 我认为这实际上是代码 公司不得不学到的一件事，当他们试图转向时，比如，为什么代码审查工具、自动代码审查工具效果不是那么好？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's actually a subtle thing, which is you push your code publicly to or publicly within your organization or your team, and then a thing roasts your code and tells you how terrible of a developer you are.
zh: 我认为实际上有一个微妙的事情，你把你的代码公开推送到或在你组织或团队内部公开推送，然后一个东西批评 你的代码并告诉你作为开发者你有多糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: Versus if you think about what Claude Code and Codex does is you're coding and then you publish the work of you plus Claude and you get bragging rights of how good of a developer you are.
zh: 与之相对的是，如果你想想 Claude Code 和 Codex 做的是什么，是你正在编码，然后你发布了你加 Claude 的工作成果，并且你获得了关于你作为开发者有多好的吹嘘 权利。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think the superhero stuff is definitely true.
zh: 所以我认为超级英雄的东西绝对是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of superhero, I wasn't planning to talk about this, but I've been hearing a lot about how much people love your agent, the Notion AI agent that you all released.
zh: 说到超级英雄，我没打算谈论这个，但我听到了很多关于人们多么喜欢你们的智能体、你们发布的 Notion AI 智能体的说法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just coming up a lot of just like, wow, this is actually really useful with a lot of different people.
zh: 它只是经常出现，就像，哇，这真的非常有用，对于很多不同的人来说。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'd be interesting to hear what you think made it so successful.
zh: 听听你认为是什么让它如此成功会很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know it was a long time before you guys launched it.
zh: 我知道在你们发布它之前已经过了很长时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like, what do you think is helping it be this useful and successful as a product out in the world?
zh: 就是说，你认为是什么帮助它变得如此有用并在世界上作为一个产品如此成功？

speaker: Unknown
en: I would like it to be even better.
zh: 我希望它变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm like my own first critic, I guess.
zh: 所以我想我是我自己的第一个批评者，我猜。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've spent most of my day thinking about where it falls short, not how great it is, but I agree with you that I'm actually surprised at how ...
zh: 我一天中的大部分时间都在思考它的不足之处，而不是它有多棒，但我同意你的观点，我实际上对……感到惊讶

speaker: Unknown
en: This sounds so weird.
zh: 这听起来很奇怪。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm surprised how good it is, if that makes sense.
zh: 我对它有多好感到惊讶，如果这说得通的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Notion has always been fairly at the forefront of AI.
zh: Notion 一直相当处于 AI 的前沿。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the first Notion Assistant was actually launched before ChatGPT.
zh: 我认为第一个 Notion Assistant 实际上是在 ChatGPT 之前推出的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's not that ...
zh: 所以并不是说……

speaker: Unknown
en: I think both Ivan and Simon had the intuition of, "Hey, this is going to change a lot of things."
zh: 我认为 Ivan 和 Simon 都有直觉，“嘿，这将改变很多事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's a huge reason why, but every company wants to become AI native now, whatever that means.
zh: 这是主要原因，但现在每家公司都想成为 AI 原生，无论这意味着什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's kind of like cloud native.
zh: 这有点像云原生。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "If you have to say it, then are you really, do you have a chance?"
zh: 我就像，“如果你非得说这个，那你真的，你有机会吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'm surprised how fast that happened for Notion, and I'll take almost no credit in this.
zh: 但我很惊讶这对 Notion 来说发生得有多快，而且我几乎不把功劳归于自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what's good about it is agents need context to operate in.
zh: 我认为它的好处在于智能体需要上下文来在其中运作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agents don't really like walls of like, "Oh, I have to go through this narrow orifice to talk to this other data repository."
zh: 智能体真的不喜欢像这样的墙壁，“噢，我得穿过这个狭窄的孔道去跟那个数据仓库说话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think for the first time, it is obvious to people why a connected workspace is actually valuable because it's great.
zh: 我认为人们第一次明显地感觉到为什么一个连通的工作空间实际上是有价值的，因为它很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can have agents roam around and do that.
zh: 我可以让智能体四处游荡并做那些事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it touches on malleable software.
zh: 这涉及到了可延展的软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think of Notion as an operating system more so.
zh: 我更多地把 Notion 视为一个操作系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then in that case, it resembles the environment that coding agents are in with Unix much more than one might maybe intuitively think.
zh: 那样的话，它就更像编码智能体所处的那个 Unix 环境，而不是人们可能直觉上认为的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think those all contribute.
zh: 所以我认为这些都促成了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then sometimes it's just we're just dumb enough to try hard things.
zh: 有时候仅仅是我们足够愚蠢去尝试困难的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think our enterprise search is like this thing where we do a lot of automatic permission handling and so on that others don't.
zh: 所以我认为我们的企业搜索就是这样，我们在那里做了很多自动权限处理等等，而其他人没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to care.
zh: 你得在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to come back to my quote from the Bible.
zh: 我要回到我引用的那句圣经经文。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like that actually is an answer to this question that it was made for such a time as this.
zh: 我感觉那实际上就是对这个问题的回答，它就是为了这样的时刻而造的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fact that Notion basically has all the things about everything in your company is the perfect source of context for using AI and helping you work.
zh: Notion 基本上拥有你公司里关于所有事情的所有信息，这一事实是使用 AI 和帮助你工作的完美上下文来源。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's just like just being around long enough for, wow, okay, this is exactly what we've been meant to be.
zh: 所以这就像是存在得足够久了，然后哇，好吧，这正是我们要被注定成为的样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a nice job.
zh: 干得漂亮。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nice job, [inaudible 00:51:19].

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the same as malleable software.
zh: 这和可延展的软件是一样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that people are waking up to malleable software now, but it's been around for a long time.
zh: 我很高兴人们现在开始意识到可延展的软件，但它已经存在很久了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was just always slightly too hard and slightly too, why would I do this?
zh: 它只是总是稍微有点太难，稍微有点太，为什么要我做这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think, yeah, I'm going to use this quote from the Bible.
zh: 所以我认为，是的，我要用这句圣经引语。

speaker: Unknown
en: Thank you.
zh: 谢谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: There it is.
zh: 就在那儿。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like shorter.
zh: 它就像更短了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The original quote is for such a time as this and interpretation is this you're like destined to do this thing.
zh: 原来的引语是 for such a time as this，解释是这就像你命中注定要做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very Bible every episode.
zh: 每一期都很有圣经味。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh man.
zh: 噢天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Going back to the way your team operates, because I think this is something that a lot of people are thinking about right now.
zh: 回到你们团队运作的方式，因为我认为这是现在很多人都在思考的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's all this talk of productivity, pace, getting things out like Anthropics launching a massive product every day, basically.
zh: 到处都是关于生产力、节奏、把东西做出来的谈论，就像 Anthropic 基本上每天都在发布一个重磅产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your job as head of product help people ship consistently, regularly, often ship great stuff.
zh: 你作为产品负责人的工作就是帮助人们持续地、定期地、经常地发布伟大的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: What has worked in allowing you guys to ship more quickly, if you are, and stuff that you're proud of, stuff that works?
zh: 什么让你们能够更快地发布，如果你们有，以及那些你们自豪的、有效的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think this answer is so specific to companies like internal culture where if you ...
zh: 我认为这个答案非常特定于像内部文化这样的公司，如果你……

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been in this situation twice in my career.
zh: 我在我的职业生涯中经历过两次这种情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is when I joined GitHub, which is obviously, I think, I don't know, insane product market fit, it just so happened that at the time that I had joined, there was a little bit of a, I don't know, identity crisis or like, "Oh, what's our next act?
zh: 一次是我加入 GitHub 时，这显然，我认为，我不知道，疯狂的产品市场契合度，只是恰好在我加入的时候，有一点，我不知道，身份危机或者像，“噢，我们的下一幕是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What do we do?"
zh: 我们该做什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And lots of debates about what to ship because it's such a tough act to follow if your first act was just incredible.
zh: 以及很多关于发布什么的争论，因为这很难接上一幕，如果你的第一幕简直不可思议的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't know, I would put Notion in the same bucket.
zh: 我不知道，我会把 Notion 归在同一类。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so in this case, it's just reminding people that, "Hey, you can just do stuff.
zh: 所以在这种情况下，只是提醒人们，“嘿，你们可以直接做事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't have to be that precious."
zh: 我们不必那么小心翼翼。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's this preciousness that develops over time.
zh: 我认为这种小心翼翼会随着时间推移而发展出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "Oh, what do we do when our users are going to be upset?"
zh: 就像，“噢，如果我们的用户要心烦意乱了我们该怎么办？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, our users are going to be upset if we don't innovate more so than if we accidentally break a thing.
zh: 嗯，如果我们不创新，我们的用户会更心烦意乱，而不是我们要是不小心搞坏了一件东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's obviously a balance, but I think just reminding people that the same group of people that was able to do the first act is very likely going to be able to do the second one, but you have to try.
zh: 所以这显然是一个平衡，但我认为只是提醒人们，能够完成第一幕的同一群人很可能也能够完成第二幕，但你得尝试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Shots on goal is a thing that we say internally a lot, which is like, great, how do you increase shots on goal?
zh: “射门次数”是我们内部常说的一件事，就像，很好，你如何增加射门次数？

speaker: Unknown
en: Which of course, if we go back to it's easier to experiment now, you're increasing the shots of on goal.
zh: 当然，如果我们回到现在实验更容易这一点，你就在增加射门的次数。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that has worked really well.
zh: 所以我认为那个真的很有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just shipping feature after feature doesn't ... We have been a little bit on a roll in terms of shipping new functionality maybe in the last, I don't know, six months or so.
zh: 只是发布一个接一个的功能并不会……我们在发布新功能方面稍微有点顺手，也许在过去，我不知道，六个月左右。

speaker: Unknown
en: But at the end of the day, feature count is the same silly metric as lines of code or tokens consumed or whatever.
zh: 但归根结底，功能数量和代码行数或消耗的 token 一样，都是愚蠢的指标。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would rather have fewer features that are really, really good and where the combinatorics let you do everything.
zh: 我宁愿要更少的功能，但那些功能真的、真的很好，并且其组合性让你能做任何事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think something that I'm still very much struggling with is software quality.
zh: 所以我认为，我仍在非常努力地挣扎的一件事是软件质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I will also say I don't think the labs are exempt from this.
zh: 我还要说，我不认为实验室能免俗。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love their tools.
zh: 我喜欢他们的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's great.
zh: 很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I live in the CLIs, but a regression every two weeks of a thing that was fixed three weeks before, and they still can't render a TUI at, I don't know, a frame rate that's reasonable.
zh: 我活在 CLI（命令行界面）里，但每两周就会出现一个三周前修复过的问题的回归，而且他们仍然无法以合理的帧率渲染 TUI。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think quality is a thing that's missing.
zh: 所以我认为质量是缺失的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: This Apple-esque machined, unibody, aluminum kind of engineering, I would like us to figure out how to get back to that as an industry.
zh: 这种苹果式的机加工、一体成型、铝制风格的工程，我希望我们作为行业能弄清楚如何回到那个状态。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there something you've done to help improve that?
zh: 你有没有做什么事情来帮助改善这一点？

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's code quality and then there's actual software quality.
zh: 所以有代码质量，然后有实际的软件质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're shipping shots on goal, there's always this balance of, okay, I would wait for it to be awesome.
zh: 如果你要发布射门尝试，总是这种平衡，好的，我会等到它变得很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know this is just very hard question to answer, but just what's your communication to the team of, here's where we're going to find that balance?
zh: 我知道这是一个很难回答的问题，但你与团队沟通的内容是，这就是我们要找到平衡的地方吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is a very frustrating thing for people, but actually, I can't show you because I'm using my laptop, but we have obviously good stickers, which is let's just only make obviously good stuff.
zh: 这是一个让人们非常沮丧的事情，但实际上，我不能展示给你看，因为我正在用我的笔记本电脑，但我们显然有很好的贴纸，上面写着让我们只做显然好的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: The origin, which is like, okay, wait, what does that mean?
zh: 其起源是，好的，等等，那是什么意思？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "Ah, you know it when you see it."
zh: 我会说，“啊，你看到它的时候你就知道了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think anyone argued when they saw the first iPhone that it's obviously good.
zh: 我不认为有人看到第一部 iPhone 时会争论它显然是好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think anyone argued that when ChatGPT first came out that it's obviously good.
zh: 我也不认为有人会在 ChatGPT 首次出现时争论它显然是好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that's the bar.
zh: 所以我认为这就是门槛。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just make obviously good stuff.
zh: 只做显然好的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the mistake that maybe a lot of companies then make is great, we're going to be in this cave in isolation until we have it be obviously good.
zh: 我认为可能许多公司随后犯的一个错误是，太好了，我们将与世隔绝地待在洞穴里，直到它变得显然好为止。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of my core values is incremental correctness, which is iterate, get really, really good at iterating.
zh: 我的核心价值观之一是渐进式正确性，即迭代，变得非常、非常擅长迭代。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I don't know, it's probably a union of, okay, increase shots on goal.
zh: 所以我不知道，这可能是两者的结合，好的，增加射门尝试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's a great example.
zh: 这是一个很好的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: We get roasted from our customers all the time, which I love about we have like six automation primitives inside of Notion if you include all the agents and so on.
zh: 我们总是被客户吐槽，我很喜欢这一点，因为我们在 Notion 里有大约六个自动化原语，如果你包括所有的智能体等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, yep, we let a bunch of different ideas grow.
zh: 我会想，是的，我们让一堆不同的想法生长。

speaker: Unknown
en: We look at how they work, but then you do have to do the hard work at consolidating it back into the naked robotic core of that idea.
zh: 我们观察它们如何工作，但随后你确实必须努力将其整合回该想法的裸露机器人核心。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's hard because you have to be okay with perhaps then shipping the next thing slightly delayed as you reconcile.
zh: 这很难，因为你必须能够接受，当你进行调和时，发布下一个东西可能会略有延迟。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we have work to do there at Notion, but as an industry too.
zh: 我认为我们在 Notion 那里还有工作要做，但作为一个行业也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like somebody was joking like, why does Claude, the desktop have three tabs of co-work, code, and I don't know what the first chat or whatever, why do we have six automation primitives?
zh: 就像有人在开玩笑，为什么 Claude 桌面版有三个 co-work、code 的标签页，我不知道第一个聊天是什么或者其他的，为什么我们有六个自动化原语？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, because someone has to sit down and reconcile them and figure out what's actually the core simple thing that should outlive the other evolutionary branches of that same idea.
zh: 嗯，因为必须有人坐下来调和它们，弄清楚什么是真正的核心简单的东西，应该比同一想法的其他进化分支寿命更长。

speaker: Unknown
en: This idea of knowing when things are obviously good, there's an element of having taste, and this is word taste that comes up a lot now, and this is like what we will need more and more because AI is building a thing now.
zh: 这种知道何时事情显然好的想法，包含品味的元素，这就是品味这个词现在出现很多的原因，这就像我们将越来越多地需要的东西，因为 AI 现在正在构建东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our job is taste.
zh: 我们的工作是品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this great?
zh: 这很棒吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this good?
zh: 这好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like you're someone that has really great taste.
zh: 我觉得你是那种有很好品味的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: A question people always ask, "How do I build taste?
zh: 人们总是问一个问题，“我如何建立品味？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I have taste?"
zh: 我有品味吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have any advice for someone that's like, "I want to develop my taste"?
zh: 你对那些想“我想培养我的品味”的人有什么建议吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: First of all, I don't know if I have great taste.
zh: 首先，我不知道我是否有很好的品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: I look at others and I look at how they exercise tastes.
zh: 我观察其他人，观察他们如何运用品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that the common thing I think is iterations with feedback.
zh: 我认为我认为共同的一点是带有反馈的迭代。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it takes a really long time to build up taste in a specific domain.
zh: 所以在特定领域建立品味需要很长时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then you maybe often can extrapolate into other domains with that taste.
zh: 然后你也许经常可以用这种品味推断到其他领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if I had to describe what taste actually means, it's you're able to run, this is such a nerdy way of describing it, you're able to run a virtual machine in your head where given an idea, you can predict for a certain in group whether they're going to like it or not.
zh: 但如果我要描述品味实际上意味着什么，就是你能够运行，这是描述它的一种极客的方式，你能够在脑海中运行一个虚拟机，给定一个想法，你能否为特定的群体预测他们是否会喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: The extremes are is, if you are the only person on the planet that thinks something is good, is it good?
zh: 极端的情况是，如果你是星球上唯一认为某个东西是好的的人，它是好的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: But maybe you also don't need to build a product for eight billion people.
zh: 但也许你也不需要为 80 亿人构建产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've never built consumer software.
zh: 我从未构建过消费者软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would probably be terrible at it, but you decide what your in group is and then how good do you get at emulating how they will react to it.
zh: 我可能会很擅长这个，但你决定了你的内部圈子是什么，然后你在模仿他们对它的反应方面做得有多好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And to do that, you just have to do reps.
zh: 要做到这一点，你只需要做重复练习。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's almost like training a model, which is also why I'm not super ... You know the whole, "Ooh, the one thing that we have left is taste."
zh: 这几乎就像训练一个模型，这也是为什么我不是超级……你知道整个，“噢，我们唯一剩下的东西就是品味。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not so sure.
zh: 我不太确定。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about the loop, it's input idea, how do people react?
zh: 如果你考虑这个循环，就是输入想法，人们如何反应？

speaker: Unknown
en: That seems very back propagation.
zh: 这看起来非常像反向传播。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: It seems very much how we train models too.
zh: 这看起来非常像我们训练模型的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I love, basically you build taste by just doing the thing, getting feedback, iterating.
zh: 所以我所喜欢的，基本上你通过做这件事，获得反馈，迭代来建立品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: Look at Japan, like Japanese craftspeople, right?
zh: 看看日本，比如日本的工匠，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: They've just been, I don't know, painting the bowl for however long and it just takes a while.
zh: 他们只是，我不知道，画这个碗很久了，这确实需要一段时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think the more reps, the increase the frequency of reps, that's what I would say.
zh: 所以我认为更多的重复练习，增加重复练习的频率，这就是我会说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so funny.
zh: 太搞笑了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's exactly how agents learn and develop how, as you said, models learn just like doing the thing, seeing was this good, was this correct?
zh: 这正是智能体学习和发展的方式，正如你所说，模型学习就像做这件事，看看这个好吗，这个正确吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, learn.
zh: 好的，学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's just doing the thing, learning, getting feedback, and there's no way to speed around this.
zh: 所以就是做这件事，学习，获得反馈，没有办法绕过这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is why often people with the best taste have been doing this for a long time.
zh: 这就是为什么通常拥有最好品味的人都已经做这件事很长时间了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The one thing I will say that I've noticed is specifically for designers, the designers that I think have, at least in software design, high taste are the ones that both have side projects that they build where they're responsible of the full thing and extent.
zh: 我会说我注意到的一件事是，特别是对于设计师来说，我认为拥有的，至少在软件设计中，高品味的设计师是那些既有他们构建的副业项目，在那里他们负责整个事情和范围。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they're also always tinkering with some new app.
zh: 而且他们也总是在摆弄一些新应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're the annoying person that is like, "Hey, what if we tried this in our team?"
zh: 他们是那种烦人的人，就像，“嘿，如果我们团队试试这个怎么样？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "Really, this is the 49th time that you suggested in a tool.
zh: 我就像，“真的，这是你第 49 次在一个工具里提出建议了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do we really need this?"
zh: 我们真的需要这个吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's exposure to other people's ideas.
zh: 这是接触其他人的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that is the ...
zh: 我认为这是……

speaker: Unknown
en: It's also really important to surround yourself with tasteful things so that you feel like the thing you are making is lacking.
zh: 把自己周围围绕着有品位的东西也是非常重要的，这样你会觉得你正在制作的东西有所欠缺。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the things we do at Notion is all of our conference rooms are named after famous objects like The first typewriter, the Macintosh, a Porsche 911 and so on.
zh: 我们在 Notion 做的一件事是，我们所有的会议室都是以著名的物体命名的，比如第一台打字机，Macintosh，保时捷 911 等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so inevitably when I'm sitting in one of the rooms and I pay attention to the room, nothing I'm doing amounts to this.
zh: 所以不可避免地，当我坐在其中一个房间里，我注意到这个房间，我所做的一切都无法与之相比。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got to do better.
zh: 我得做得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've built so many successful, great, loved products.
zh: 你建立了这么多成功的、伟大的、受人喜爱的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you think matters in the end to building a successful product if you had to just boil it down?
zh: 如果你必须提炼一下，你认为最终对于建立一个成功的产品来说重要的是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's the one trick that I'll sell a course next week.
zh: 这是我要下周卖课程的一个诀窍。

speaker: Unknown
en: Please, I'll sign up.
zh: 拜托，我会报名的。

speaker: Unknown
en: First of all, I think I would actually say that I have contributed to some really great products, not built them because I think I did not use to believe this early on in my career, but the longer I'm in this, the more I care about what's the team that's building the thing.
zh: 首先，我想我实际上会说，我为一些真正伟大的产品做出了贡献，而不是构建了它们，因为我以前并不相信这一点，在我职业生涯的早期，但我在这个行业待得越久，我就越关心构建这个东西的团队是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I used to think that was such a, I don't know, not important thing.
zh: 我过去认为那是一个，我不知道，不重要的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now I'm like, oh, it's the only thing.
zh: 现在我就像，哦，这是唯一的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think that there is a through line out of the things that I've contributed to where I can pinpoint it.
zh: 我不认为在我所贡献的事情中有一条主线，让我可以指出它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that you can't say that the best design always wins.
zh: 我认为你不能说最好的设计总是赢家。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's many products where just design doesn't matter.
zh: 我认为有很多产品只是设计根本不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think then as a designer, you can have this identity crisis of like, why am I doing this?
zh: 我认为然后作为一个设计师，你会有这种身份危机，就像，我为什么要做这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you can't even say that the way it's built always, the best engineering always wins.
zh: 我认为你甚至不能说它的构建方式总是，最好的工程总是赢家。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think one of the biggest pitfalls is if you get into the loop of, if I just add one more thing to the product, it'll be finally great.
zh: 我认为最大的陷阱之一是，如果你陷入了这个循环，如果我再给产品加一个东西，它终于会很棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: If I really look at the truly great products, they all have one tiny core that is so exceptionally good.
zh: 如果我真的看那些真正伟大的产品，它们都有一个非常小的核心，是极其出色的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is both a combination of you stumbled upon it by luck and then the market agreed.
zh: 这是运气偶然发现它然后市场同意的结合。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think it's the, what's the tiny core?
zh: 但我认为这是，那个微小的核心是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, multi-touch on the phone.
zh: 我不知道，手机上的多点触控。

speaker: Unknown
en: GitHub is probably the poll request.
zh: GitHub 可能是 pull request。

speaker: Unknown
en: This idea that anyone can suggest something to you and you see it.
zh: 这个想法是任何人都可以向你建议一些东西，然后你看到它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do think that at Notion, it's the blocks and the slash commands.
zh: 我确实认为在 Notion，是 blocks 和斜杠命令。

speaker: Unknown
en: Figma, it's the seamless blend between real-time collaboration and not.
zh: Figma，它是实时协作与非实时协作之间的无缝融合。

speaker: Unknown
en: All the great products have something tiny that is a superpower that's like versus, oh yeah, if we have this suite of things and we add one more thing, it'll finally be useful.
zh: 所有伟大的产品都有一个微小的东西是一种超能力，就像，哦耶，如果我们有这一套东西，然后再加一个东西，它终于会有用了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That never works.
zh: 那从不管用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for GitHub, interesting, it was the PR.
zh: 对于 GitHub，有趣的是，它是 PR。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are there examples of that at other places you worked?
zh: 你工作过的其他地方有这种例子吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because this is really interesting, just like what's the tiny core that makes everything else work?
zh: 因为这真的很有趣，就像是什么是让其他一切都能运作的微小核心？

speaker: Unknown

en: And I'm like, who cares if some machine is better at it?

en: It's the human stuff.

en: And so I think I would do the same thing.

speaker: Unknown
en: I would tinker.

speaker: Unknown
en: I would build stuff.

en: I would try and make the world around me more malleable.

speaker: Unknown
en: But yeah, I don't know, something like that.

speaker: Unknown
en: Just I would do the same thing.

speaker: Unknown
en: Amazing.

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.

en: I'm going to take us to two recurring corners of the podcast to see what we find there.

speaker: Unknown
en: The first corner is contrarian corner.

en: Is there something that you have a ... You have a lot of these already.

speaker: Unknown
en: I will have to caveat this with, if you're McDonald's and you have exclusive hiring practices and it's the only job in a location, that is not what I'm talking about.
zh: 我必须先加个说明，如果你是麦当劳，你有排他性的招聘做法，而且是那个地点唯一的雇主，那不是我在说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But going back to comfy, air-conditioned job kind of thing is like, great, just work with and for the top of the class is sometimes a winning thing and just build a really, really good product for them, which by definition means you're going to exclude others.
zh: 但回到那种舒适的、有空调的工作这类事情，就像，很好，只要与顶级人才共事、为他们工作，有时就是一种制胜之道，只是为他们构建一个真正、真正好的产品，这根据定义意味着你将排斥其他人。

speaker: Unknown
en: The TPPN guys have a really good way of describing this exact concept, which is they had like, I don't know, 8,000 listeners and a conversation.
zh: TPPN 的人有一种很好的方式来描述这个确切的概念，就是他们有，我不知道，8000 个听众和一场对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: They got acquired for hundreds of millions of dollars, just like what's going on there?
zh: 他们被数亿美元收购了，就像那里发生了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And the way they pitch it is, if we have millions of people listening to this thing, we've done something wrong.
zh: 而他们推介的方式是，如果我们有数百万人听这东西，我们就做错了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is specifically designed for the people in power of tech to influence them, to teach them what's going on, and it worked out.
zh: 这是专门为科技界的掌权者设计的，以影响他们，教给他们正在发生什么，而且奏效了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It worked out great for them.
zh: 这对他们来说结果非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's exactly what you're describing.
zh: 所以这正如你描述的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, I'm going to take us now to fail corner.
zh: 好的，我现在带大家进入失败角落。

speaker: Unknown
en: So people like you come on this podcast, they're like, "Okay, I'll get all these wonderful things."
zh: 所以像你这样的人来到这个播客，他们就像，“好的，我会得到所有这些美好的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's done and he's just killing it all the time.
zh: 他做完了，他一直都在大杀四方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything's working.
zh: 一切都很顺利。

speaker: Unknown
en: In reality, I'm sure not everything has worked in the course of your career.
zh: 实际上，我确信在你的职业生涯中并非事事都顺利。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's one example where things didn't work out and what did you learn from that experience?
zh: 有哪一件事没做成的例子，你从那次经历中学到了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh my God, it's such a weird ...
zh: 噢天哪，这是一个奇怪的……

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think about win versus fail.
zh: 我不考虑赢还是输。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like every day I fail a lot.
zh: 我觉得我每天都在失败很多次。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are big ones that annoy me?
zh: 有哪些大失败让我感到恼火？

speaker: Unknown
en: Culturally, I think in running teams, I think a mistake that I made is at some point because hiring ... Now, it's easy, but at the time hiring designers that can code was quite challenging.
zh: 在文化上，我认为在管理团队时，我觉得我犯的一个错误是在某个时候，因为招聘……现在很容易，但在当时招聘会写代码的设计师是相当具有挑战性的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so then if you loosen that requirement, I did not predict how quickly that becomes like a slippery slope.
zh: 所以如果你放宽那个要求，我没有预料到那会多么快地变成一种滑坡效应。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I would rather have had fewer designers that are more polymath.
zh: 我宁愿有更少数量的设计师，他们是更多面手。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that's one on organizational side.
zh: 所以我认为那是组织方面的一个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: On product, oh my God, I mean GitHub Actions and they're the ...
zh: 在产品上，噢天哪，我是说 GitHub Actions 还有它们是……

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, it's very technical, but the fact that we also thought we didn't need good package management for the actions.
zh: 我不知道，这很技术化，但事实上我们也认为我们不需要为 actions 提供好的包管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the world would be better off if we had thought about that slightly harder.
zh: 我想如果我们稍微多思考一下那一点，世界会变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is maybe like I started a competitor to Notion in 2014 and I didn't think of it as ...
zh: 这也许就像我在 2014 年启动了一个 Notion 的竞争对手，而且我不认为它是……

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, it wasn't a competitor of Notion because the week that we were going to get a term sheet from True Ventures, Notion pivoted from website building to document collaboration.
zh: 事实上，它不是 Notion 的竞争对手，因为我们要从 True Ventures 获得投资意向书的那一周，Notion 从网站建设转型到了文档协作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so True Ventures was like, "Hey, sorry, we have a conflict."
zh: 所以 True Ventures 说，“嘿，抱歉，我们要利益冲突。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're like, "Yep, no worries."
zh: 我们说，“是的，别担心。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And we spent so much time polishing the editing experience.
zh: 我们花了这么多时间打磨编辑体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: We did markdown folding, all the stuff that you now have in Obsidian, we did that back in 2014, and we thought that's the thing that really matters.
zh: 我们做了 markdown 折叠，所有你现在在 Obsidian 里有的东西，我们在 2014 年就做了，我们以为那是真正重要的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then Notion, by comparison, the first version of the Notion editor was terrible.
zh: 相比之下 Notion，Notion 编辑器的第一个版本很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was all blocks.
zh: 全是块。

speaker: Unknown
en: You couldn't even select between two blocks, but it turns out it didn't matter.
zh: 你甚至不能在两个块之间选择，但结果证明这并不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that is just working diligently on the wrong thing for way too long, huge fail.
zh: 所以我认为那只是在错误的事情上勤奋地工作了太长时间，巨大的失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's so interesting.
zh: 那太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just coming back to your insight of when a product works, there's just this tiny core thing that is the thing that makes it amazing and what people want to come back to no matter how bad everything else is.
zh: 回到你关于产品何时奏效的洞察，就是只有这个微小的核心东西，正是让产品变得神奇、让人们无论其他部分多糟糕都想回来的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's a really interesting takeaway.
zh: 我认为这是一个非常有趣的收获。

speaker: Unknown
en: We actually kept adding new features.
zh: 我们实际上持续添加新功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: At some point you go down to the death spiral, so we kept adding yet another feature of like, "Okay, is it good now?
zh: 在某个点你会进入死亡螺旋，所以我们持续添加又一个功能，就像，“好的，现在好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it good now?"
zh: 现在好了吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's just, nope, the core wasn't good.
zh: 只是，不，核心不够好。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's interesting.
zh: 有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in your experience, you can tell pretty quickly, "Okay.
zh: 根据你的经验，你可以很快分辨出来，“好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow.
zh: 哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: This has really taken off.
zh: 这真的起飞了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We found something really powerful here."
zh: 我们在这里发现了一些真正强大的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you can tell.
zh: 我想你能分辨。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think it's the obviously good thing.
zh: 是的，我认为这显然是好事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you're like, "Yep, this is good."
zh: 我想你会说，“是的，这很好。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it may be good in a way that you give it to users and every single user study that you do or whatever, just it falls flat and they don't know how to use it.
zh: 然后它可能在某种意义上很好，当你把它给用户，你做的每一次用户研究或者别的什么，就是毫无反响，他们不知道怎么用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the important thing is actually to not give up on the core idea.
zh: 我认为重要的是实际上不要放弃核心理念。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's 80%, but then the 20% is relentlessly iterate until it actually clicks with the folks that you're working for.
zh: 所以那是 80%，但剩下的 20% 是无情地迭代，直到它真的与你为之工作的人产生共鸣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Max, is there anything else that you wanted to share with folks?
zh: Max，你还有什么想和大家分享的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else you want to leave listeners with before we get to our very exciting lightning round?
zh: 在我们进入非常激动人心的闪电轮之前，还有什么想留给听众的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: When I talk to young ...
zh: 当我和年轻……

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so funny to say that, but younger people in Silicon Valley.
zh: 这么说很有趣，但是硅谷里的年轻人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right now, I think that Silicon Valley is uncharacteristically full of people who don't actually love computers.
zh: 我认为目前硅谷不同寻常地充满了那些实际上并不热爱电脑的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I mean by that is it's like, "Oh, I want to make money."
zh: 我的意思是说，就像，“哦，我想赚钱。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course everybody does.
zh: 当然每个人都想。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like making money too.
zh: 我也喜欢赚钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's this idea of this is the last train or what do we pose, the permanent underclass kind of stuff.
zh: 我认为有一种想法，这是末班车或者我们要怎么假设，那种永久的底层阶级之类的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it is so detrimental to thinking about how you want to spend your heartbeats in life.
zh: 这对思考你想在生命中如何花费你的心跳是非常有害的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I don't know, except ...
zh: 所以我不知道，除了……

speaker: Unknown
en: The advice I would give is just don't let the rush or the frenzy distract you from the things that you actually care about and are passionate in life.
zh: 我会给出的建议就是，不要让匆忙或狂热分散你对你真正关心和充满激情的事情的注意力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it'll find a way.
zh: 我认为它会找到出路。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that is not to mean that you shouldn't work hard.
zh: 这并不是说你不应该努力工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think you're actually way better off if you work incredibly hard from 18 to 25 or whatever.
zh: 我认为如果你从 18 岁到 25 岁或者不管什么年纪极其努力地工作，你实际上会过得好得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the way to go.
zh: 那才是正道。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should work a lot and then later, you can work a little less.
zh: 你应该多工作，然后以后，你可以少工作一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's more about the frenetic nature.
zh: 所以更多是关于那种疯狂的本质。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're so worried that if you don't win, if you don't take that last train out, you're going to be screwed.
zh: 你太担心如果你不赢，如果你没有赶上那班末班车，你就完蛋了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it doesn't seem right to me.
zh: 这对我来说似乎不对。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it seems like a very hollow way of leading life.
zh: 我认为这似乎是一种非常空虚的生活方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I would encourage people to zoom out and not think about it that way.
zh: 所以我鼓励人们拉远镜头，不要那样思考。

speaker: Unknown
en: Read history, read computer science history maybe.
zh: 读读历史，也许读读计算机科学历史。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's easy to hear that and feel like, "Okay, I'll be all right.
zh: 听到那个很容易感觉像，“好吧，我会没事的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just going to work on things that I'm excited about."
zh: 我就只去做我兴奋的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then like, "Okay, but how will I actually have a job in the future?"
zh: 然后就像，“好吧，但我未来怎么才能真正找到工作？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I love the sentiment.
zh: 我喜欢这种观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't be so stressed about missing out on things and being in the permanent underclass.
zh: 不要对错过事情和成为永久底层阶级感到有压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything there that you think is important for people to do while not being overly stressed and worried about missing that train?
zh: 你认为在不至于过度紧张和担心错过那趟车的情况下，有什么事情是人们做的重要的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if it's Chris Rock, but there's a comedian that has this joke that is like, "It's great to follow your passion."
zh: 我不知道是不是 Chris Rock，但有一个喜剧演员有个笑话就像，“追随你的激情很棒。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then he has this pause and is like, "If it pays."
zh: 然后他停顿了一下就像，“如果它有钱赚的话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so obviously, there is a little bit to that.
zh: 所以显然，这有一点道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not suggesting that you don't worry about this at all.
zh: 我不是建议你完全不用担心这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it's more that just tune down the amplitude of how much worry there is and then just realizing that history repeats itself more so than it is completely novel and new.
zh: 我认为更多是调低担忧的幅度，然后意识到历史会重演，而不是完全新颖和全新的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then of course, yeah, if you tie it to agency and if you're not so stuck in, "Oh, I need certainty of how the world is going to unfold," you're probably going to be fine.
zh: 当然，是的，如果你把它联系到智能体，如果你不是那么执着于，“哦，我需要世界如何展开的确定性，”你可能会没事的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in the extreme, this is the other side of things, which is often if I then talk to people who are like, "Yep, but everything's going to change."
zh: 在极端情况下，这是事情的另一面，这通常是我如果和那些说，“是的，但一切都会改变”的人交谈时。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Okay, great.
zh: 我就像，“好吧，很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So how is a move that you are going to make really going to shield you from it?
zh: 所以你要做出的举动真的能让你从中幸免吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And do you want to live in a society where all of this ..." I don't know, it just seems so insular, that mindset.
zh: 你想生活在一个所有这一切……的社会吗？” 我不知道，那种心态似乎太孤立了。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, we have reached our very exciting lightning round.
zh: 说到这，我们已经到了我们非常激动人心的闪电轮。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got five questions for you.
zh: 我有五个问题要问你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you ready?
zh: 你准备好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sure.
zh: 当然。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are two or three books that you find yourself recommending most to other people?
zh: 有哪两三本书是你发现自己最经常推荐给别人的？

speaker: Unknown
en: It depends on the person.
zh: 这取决于人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say ...
zh: 我会说……

speaker: Unknown
en: So Code by Charles Petzold, which is The Secret Language of Hardware and Software.
zh: 所以 Charles Petzold 写的《Code》，也就是《硬件与软件的秘密语言》。

speaker: Unknown
en: It basically is like, "Do you know how computers actually work?"
zh: 它基本上就像是，“你知道计算机实际上是如何工作的吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: It is actually surprising to me how many professionally employed programmers don't know how computers work.
zh: 令我惊讶的是，实际上有多少专业在职的程序员不知道计算机是如何工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That one, the funny thing is it does not have a line of code in it until chapter 27, so exceptionally good book.
zh: 那一本，有趣的是它直到第 27 章才有一行代码，所以是一本非常好的书。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a weird one, which is Tools of Conviviality by Ivan Illich.
zh: 我有一个奇怪的推荐，是 Ivan Illich 的《Tools of Conviviality》。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the contrast between, you look at the history of technology and tools that let users exercise human ingenuity and autonomy versus tools that are more at industrial scale that almost have become destructive to human autonomy.
zh: 这是两者之间的对比，你回顾一下技术史，那些让用户发挥人类聪明才智和自主性的工具，与那些更多处于工业规模、几乎已对人类自主性产生破坏作用的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the last one that I give mostly to executives that I think are creating a lot of systems is Seeing Like a State, which I think there is a famous stack overflow that popularized this, but it's the idea of, are you actually just designing a system so that you have legibility, but the way that you've created that legibility completely neglects the reality of the system on the ground.
zh: 然后我给出的最后一个推荐，主要是给那些我认为正在构建很多系统的高管的，是《Seeing Like a State》，我认为有一个著名的 stack overflow 推广了它，但它的理念是，你实际上只是在设计一个系统以使其具有可读性，但你创造这种可读性的方式完全忽视了系统中现实情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think of it as, great, you're the executive and you have these status reports and you think you know exactly how your teams work.
zh: 所以我把它看作是，好吧，你是高管，你有这些状态报告，你认为你确切知道你的团队是如何工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you actually spend time with the teams, you would realize that none of that is actually true.
zh: 如果你实际上花时间与团队在一起，你会意识到这些都不是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think executives love creating fake legibility for themselves because we don't like noise as humans, right?
zh: 所以我认为高管们喜欢为自己创造虚假的可读性，因为作为人类我们不喜欢噪音，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: We want the signal, but there's often less signal in it than one might think.
zh: 我们想要信号，但其中的信号往往比人们想象的要少。

speaker: Unknown
en: Favorite recent movie or TV show that you have recently enjoyed?
zh: 你最近喜欢的最近的电影或电视剧是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I have purposeful, terrible taste in movies, which is I want to watch movies that I never think about again after watching them.
zh: 我在电影方面有故意的、糟糕的品味，也就是我想看那些看过之后我再也不会去想的电影。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just want to be entertained and I mostly just want to see things that I couldn't remotely experience in real life.
zh: 我只是想被娱乐，而且我主要只是想看那些我在现实生活中无法体验的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you should not ask me for movie recommendations.
zh: 所以你不应该问我电影推荐。

speaker: Unknown
en: I did like Project Hail Mary a lot.
zh: 我真的很喜欢《挽救计划》。

speaker: Unknown
en: I liked the book and I think the adaptation was really good.
zh: 我喜欢这本书，我认为改编真的很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it also makes me super excited about any kind of future of humanity, which is I sometimes joke to our teams internally, which is like, okay, if we're really, really good at some point and Notion OS will be the thing that empowers five to eight people explore the galaxy somehow and everything will be organized for them in Notion.
zh: 我认为这也让我对人类的任何未来感到超级兴奋，我有时会在内部跟我们的团队开玩笑，就像这样，好吧，如果我们真的在某个时候做得非常好，并且 Notion OS 将成为那个能够赋能 5 到 8 个人探索银河系的东西，而且一切都会在 Notion 中为他们组织好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I like this idea of pushing into space.
zh: 我喜欢这个向太空推进的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: TV show, I'm late to this, The Handmaid's Tale.
zh: 电视剧方面，我晚了点才看《使女的故事》。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you replace the concept of God with AI in that TV show, and then you don't actually have to squint that far to replace eyes with ICE in that TV show right now, it becomes a very, I don't know, the heavy show to watch in a good way.
zh: 如果你在那部电视剧中把上帝的概念替换成 AI，然后你实际上不用太费劲就能把那里的眼睛替换成现在的 ICE，它就变成了一部非常，我不知道，以某种好的方式来说很沉重的剧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Wow, I had not thought about that.
zh: 哇，我没想到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Under His Eye, was that one of the things?
zh: “愿主庇佑”，那是其中一件事吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Under his AI.
zh: “在他的 AI 之下”。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whoa.
zh: 哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not afraid.
zh: 我不害怕。

speaker: Unknown
en: I used to watch it and I'm more afraid to watch it now.
